summary: Iter 222 — Phase B.0 Workflow State Observer de-risking pre-flight. Backend-only observability shim. All acceptance criteria met: (1) 5/5 new pytests in tests/test_workflow_state_observer.py pass (real-caller attribution, never-raises-on-db-error, summary groups by caller+doc_type, clamps days, recent list filters+limits). (2) Full 63-test learning-core regression suite passes (test_weekly_digest, test_u6_so_telemetry, test_unified_feedback, test_learning_core_events, test_pattern_health_core, test_drift_and_fingerprint, test_intake_learning_feedback, test_cold_start_matcher, test_vendor_profile_helpers). (3) Backend imports cleanly, supervisor RUNNING, no 'WorkflowObserver record failed' in backend.err.log. (4) GET /api/admin/workflow-observer/summary?days=7 returns empty-state zeros correctly; after triggering a real record_workflow_call, summary aggregates correctly (total_calls, by_caller, by_doc_type, by_caller_x_doc_type). (5) GET /api/admin/workflow-observer/recent?limit=5 returns {total, observations[]} with no _id leakage, and ?caller_func=foo filter narrows correctly. (6) GET /api/admin/workflow-observer/recent?limit=9999 → 422 as expected. (7) TTL index confirmed: `ttl_created_at` on created_at with expireAfterSeconds=2592000 (30 days), plus by_caller + by_doc_type indexes. (8) Stack-walk skip logic verified live: triggered record from `my_test_caller_function` and caller_func came back as 'my_test_caller_function' (NOT '_update_standard_workflow_status'). (9) Observation shape verified: id(uuid), doc_id, doc_type, confidence (float rounded 4dp), has_normalized_fields(bool), caller_file, caller_func, caller_line, created_at (ISO UTC), week_key ('2026-W16'). (10) Regression endpoints responsive: /api/health 200, /api/sales-dashboard/summary 200, /api/intake/learning/summary 200, /api/documents 200, /api/learning/digest/latest 200.

minor.issue: Spec bullet says 'days=999 clamps to 90; days=0 clamps to 1' but the router declares days as Query(7, ge=1, le=90) so out-of-range values return HTTP 422 instead of being clamped. The service layer (get_observer_summary) DOES clamp via max(1, min(days, 90)) and the unit test test_summary_clamps_days_range exercises that service-level clamp (passes). So one of two decisions for the main agent: (a) keep Query bounds (HTTP 422 is consistent with /recent?limit=9999 → 422) and update the spec wording, or (b) drop Query bounds and rely on the service-level clamp. Current behaviour is self-consistent — just flagging the spec mismatch.

action_items: Decide spec vs. implementation for days bounds on /summary endpoint: either (a) keep Query(ge=1,le=90) + 422 on out-of-range and adjust the spec bullet, OR (b) remove the bounds and let the service-layer clamp apply at HTTP layer. Unit test currently validates the service-level clamp, not the HTTP layer.
critical_code_review_comments: services/workflow_state_observer.py (167 lines): clean, well-documented, fire-and-forget semantics enforced via try/except around the full record_workflow_call body. Index creation is idempotent with a module-level _INDEXES_READY guard. Good.

Stack-walk logic in _caller_frame correctly skips both (a) frames from this_file (the observer module itself) and (b) any frame whose function name is in SKIP_FUNCS ({'_update_standard_workflow_status'}). Verified live — caller attribution returns the real caller. One small risk worth documenting but not fixing now: if _update_standard_workflow_status is ever renamed or extracted to a different module, the SKIP_FUNCS set must be updated in lockstep. Consider passing the wrapped function name as a parameter to record_workflow_call to eliminate the magic string — a trivial hardening for Phase B.

routers/workflow_observer.py (38 lines): minimal, correctly prefixed with /admin/workflow-observer, uses get_db() dependency, excludes _id in responses (via the service's projection). Query bounds on /summary (ge=1, le=90) and /recent (ge=1, le=500) are both enforced — /recent?limit=9999 → 422 confirmed.

server.py L2020-2030 instrumentation: fire-and-forget try/except wraps the await, late-imports record_workflow_call (avoids cold-start cost if observer is disabled), placed BEFORE the find_one early-return so every invocation is observed regardless of whether the doc exists. Correct.

tests/test_workflow_state_observer.py (157 lines): good coverage of the 5 acceptance criteria. FakeDb/FakeColl stand-ins are concise and match the Motor async API surface the observer uses (insert_one, find with projection+sort+to_list, create_index). Nice.
No files exceed 700 lines. No new imports bloat. No new env vars. Observer is entirely non-blocking and collection is self-bounded by the 30-day TTL.
test_credentials: admin/admin (not exercised — observer endpoints currently unauthenticated admin paths)
seed_data_creation: Wrote 1 temporary observation row (doc_id='TEST_ITER222_observer_trigger') to workflow_state_observations to verify end-to-end caller-attribution + no-_id + index behaviour against real MongoDB, then deleted it (delete_many returned 1). Did NOT touch C-DEMO-OVRD-1, 2026-W15 digest, or Giovanni C-10250. Collection now back to count=0 rows; indexes remain in place.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 222 Phase B.0 observability shim is verified green. The only open item is the minor spec/impl mismatch on days bounds for /summary (422 vs clamp) — flagged for the main agent. TTL=30 days on created_at is live. To rerun the observer regression: `cd /app/backend && pytest tests/test_workflow_state_observer.py tests/test_weekly_digest.py tests/test_u6_so_telemetry.py tests/test_unified_feedback.py tests/test_learning_core_events.py tests/test_pattern_health_core.py tests/test_drift_and_fingerprint.py tests/test_intake_learning_feedback.py tests/test_cold_start_matcher.py tests/test_vendor_profile_helpers.py --tb=short` → expects 68 passed. Phase B proper (actually moving the 427-line _update_standard_workflow_status) will land in a later iteration — observer is ready to capture production caller data until then. Pre-existing unrelated noise in backend.err.log: BC Azure-AD tenant token failures (ignore).
rca of the issue: N/A — observability pre-flight verification pass, no bug fix. The one minor finding (spec says clamp, router says 422) is a documentation/intent question, not a defect.
